title: Agalinis fasciculata
intro: Agalinis fasciculata, commonly known as fascicled twining blue gentian, is a perennial plant species in the flowering plant family Orobanchaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory problems, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Agalinis fasciculata include alkaloids, such as agalinine, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Agalinis fasciculata is commonly prepared as a tincture, tea, or infused oil to support immune function and alleviate respiratory symptoms.
taxonomy: Agalinis fasciculata is classified in the family Orobanchaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as broomrapes or Indian paintbrushes. Agalinis fasciculata, also known as clustered agalinis, is a species of flowering plant native to North America. It is a type of hemiparasitic plant, which means it obtains some of its nutrients by parasitizing other plants. Agalinis fasciculata is further classified within the genus Agalinis and the order Lamiales.
